column1: # decision D-8 "minimum en/de"). Driven through FormatCtx::new(Date1900,
# Locale::DeDe) by sheet-conformance/tests/locale.rs. The format CODE is
# locale-NEUTRAL (same `#,##0.00` / `mmmm` / `AM/PM` tokens as the en goldens);
# only the RENDERED separators, month/day names, and AM/PM strings differ. The
# matching en-US goldens (number/datetime.golden.tsv) MUST stay byte-identical —
# this file is the de mirror, never an en regression.
# ---- ampm (sheet.format.locale.ampm): de honors the AM/PM token literally ----
# RULING: German Excel does NOT localize the explicit AM/PM token to vorm./
# nachm. — when the format CODE contains AM/PM, the rendered marker is the en
# "AM"/"PM" string in de-DE too (the de table mirrors en for am/pm). The 24h
# convention is honored by AUTHORS dropping the AM/PM token (`HH:mm`), not by
# the engine substituting German strings into an AM/PM code.
